name: The Silent Strikes
description: The Silent has moved against Vane. His investigation has been flagged and an Inquisitor authorized. Kira needs to know tonight — not tomorrow. The window for simultaneous transmission is narrow.
accept_text: Vane is at the window when you arrive, which he usually isn't — he works at the desk, he doesn't stand at windows. He turns when you enter and his expression has the quality of a man who has been expecting a problem and has found it. 'I have six hours,' he says without preamble. 'Possibly four. An Inquisitor authorization came through this morning — no case number, no filing chain, the office code is the same syntactically-correct-nonexistent office that has been appearing in Lazarus authorization records for the last year.' He crosses to the desk and opens the small ledger, the one with no Ministry crest. 'They know I've been investigating. I don't know how — I've been careful. But they know.' He closes the ledger. 'I can move the evidence through Ministry channels. The timing has to match Kira's Skyborn transmission exactly — if I file first, the classification comes down before the Aetherian government receives it. If Kira transmits first, the Ministry has a chance to discredit it as Aetherian intelligence.' He meets your eyes. 'Simultaneous. Both governments at the same moment. The Silent can suppress one channel. They cannot suppress both at once.' He picks up the ledger. 'Get to Kira. Tell her tonight. Tell her the window is two minutes past midnight — I've already staged the filing. When she confirms, I move.' A pause. 'Go now. The Inquisitor moves at dawn.'
complete_text: Kira runs a calibration when you board, which she always does, because priorities are priorities. When it completes she turns and you tell her what Vane said. She is quiet for a moment — the evaluation look that she uses for problems that are genuinely worth evaluating. 'Simultaneous,' she says. Not a question. 'Two minutes past midnight.' She checks the navigational crystal. 'My relay window to the Aetherian high command is open from midnight to four. I can transmit in that window without Ministry classification blocking it.' She runs a second calibration that she doesn't need to run. 'The SCAMP evidence — the testimony from the last engineer. The commissioning document Morlak found. All of it?' You tell her yes. She is still for a moment. 'Three hundred years,' she says. Not to you. 'They commissioned the weapon that made the war permanent. Three hundred years and it's been sitting in a sealed case in a dead man's investigation file.' She looks at the sky — her sky, the one she knows from above. 'The war doesn't end tonight.' She says it directly, making sure you understand this. 'The Aetherian government receives documentation that their predecessors were complicit in permanent war. They can deny it, investigate it, bury it.' She pauses. 'Or they can hold it. Let it do what facts do when they're out — accumulate context, find witnesses, grow.' She looks at you. 'That's all this is. A fact getting out. Everything after that is people deciding what to do with a fact.' She turns back to the crystal. 'Tell Vane: two minutes past midnight. If he's transmitting, I'm transmitting. Go.'
